contentBlocks.original:
वागर्थाविव सम्प्रुक्तौ वागर्थप्रतिपत्तये
जगतः पितरौ वन्दे पार्वतीपरमेश्वरौ
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vāgarthāviva sampruktau vāgarthapratipattaye
jagataḥ pitarau vande pārvatīparameśvarau
contentBlocks.translation:
I worship Parvati and Parameshwara, the parents of the world, who are inseparable, just like a word and its sense, in order to guide me in acquiring the right understanding of words and their meanings.

Alternate meaning when Parvathi Paramaeshvarau is considered as Parvathipa Rameshvaru: Kalidasa is praying to Lord Shiva and Lord Vishnu who are ‘Pitharau’ (fathers) to this Jagath who are inseparable like the word and the meaning.
contentBlocks.meanings: वागर्थौ (vāgarthau) – word and meaning (vāk:word | artha:meaning); इव (iva) – like; सम्प्रुक्तौ (sampruktau) – inseparably joined; वागर्थप्रतिपत्तये (vāgarthapratipattaye) – for understanding words and meanings (vāk:word | artha:meaning | pratipatti:understanding | aye:for); जगतः (jagataḥ) – of the world; पितरौ (pitarau) – parents; वन्दे (vande) – I worship; पार्वतीपरमेश्वरौ (pārvatīparameśvarau) – Parvati and Parameshwara (pārvatī:Parvati | parameśvara:Parameshwara);
